overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. With a cumulative following of 3,580, his posts consistently generate meaningful interactions, driven by professional milestones, tool releases, and academic opportunities. Bluesky yields the highest relative engagement rate, while LinkedIn serves as the primary driver for broad, professional reach.

contentThemes.description: Research findings, tools like CooRnet, and methodologies related to identifying online manipulation, link sharing, and coordinated campaigns.
Announcements regarding postdoctoral openings, PhD opportunities, and prestigious research grant awards.
Discussions, conference presentations, and methodological approaches to studying platform data.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's highly active academic community by sharing immediate research results and paper releases there first to maximize viral potential.
Optimize LinkedIn's large follower base of 1,850 by formatting recruitment and grant posts with direct links and clear, actionable instructions.
Improve discoverability on Mastodon and Bluesky by consistently incorporating relevant academic and tool-specific hashtags.
Capitalize on high engagement rates by initiating follow-up discussions or Q&A sessions in the comments of top-performing posts.

platformInsights.bluesky: Demonstrates the highest average engagement rate (6.14%) among all platforms, driven by an active academic network that responds exceptionally well to paper publications and opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: An engaged, specialized audience. Tool updates like CooRnet v2.0 perform well, though overall reach is structurally limited by the lower follower count.
platformInsights.linkedin: Serves as the largest audience hub (1,850 followers) and generates the highest absolute engagement, making it highly effective for institutional announcements, hiring, and grant wins.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
